checks.evidence: The artifact directory contains exactly one file: health-permissions-review.html. It embeds its theme and component CSS and contains no external URLs, imports, scripts, or asset references.

checks.evidence: The exact long title and “ready for review” status appear at lines 429-430. The title uses a flexible minimum-width layout and switches to a block layout below 680px at lines 238-258.
checks.label: pass

checks.evidence: Lines 495-523 contain exactly five ordered steps. CSS lines 281-335 render them as a responsive grid on wide screens and a single vertical connected sequence below 680px.
checks.label: pass
checks.name: five_step_linear_path

checks.evidence: The risk comparison is a labeled table at lines 534-568. CSS lines 359-409 provide internal table scrolling on wide screens and stacked, data-label rows below 680px; page-level horizontal overflow is clipped by the base layout.
checks.label: pass
checks.name: mobile_risk_readability

checks.evidence: Lines 437-438 and 492 state that HealthKit is an import source and SQLite remains canonical, with no schema or migration changes. Lines 526-531 mark the 14 tests and 9-to-3 scope reduction as confirmed, while lines 573-577 separately identify the denied-sleep simulator check as remaining and condition approval on it.
checks.label: pass
